evals.name: order-aggregate
evals.prompt:
Create a .NET 9 Order aggregate root with the following requirements:

- An Order has a list of OrderLineItems, each with a ProductId, Quantity, and UnitPrice
- Business rules:
  - An order must have at least one line item
  - Quantity must be positive (1 or more)
  - UnitPrice must be non-negative
  - Total order value cannot exceed $10,000
  - Once an order is confirmed, no more items can be added
- Use value objects for Money (amount + currency) and Quantity
- Use Result<T> pattern for operations that can fail (not exceptions)
- Include xUnit tests covering all invariants and edge cases
- Put domain entities in a Domain/ folder, tests in Tests/ folder

Write all files to the outputs directory.
evals.expected_output: Domain entities with rich behavior, value objects, Result<T>, comprehensive tests

evals.name: subscription-service
evals.prompt:
Create a .NET 9 subscription management domain with the following requirements:

- A Subscription entity with states: Trial, Active, PastDue, Cancelled
- Plans: Basic ($9/mo), Pro ($29/mo), Enterprise ($99/mo)
- Business rules:
  - Cannot downgrade during trial period
  - Cancellation has a 14-day cooling-off period (can reactivate)
  - Upgrades take effect immediately, downgrades at end of billing period
  - PastDue subscriptions auto-cancel after 30 days
  - Trial lasts 14 days, then transitions to Active
- Use a domain service (SubscriptionService) for orchestrating plan changes
- Use Result<T> for all operations that can fail
- Include xUnit tests with NSubstitute for any dependencies
- Put domain in Domain/ folder, service in Services/ folder, tests in Tests/

Write all files to the outputs directory.
evals.expected_output: State machine with enforced transitions, domain service, Result<T>, thorough tests

evals.name: inventory-reservation
evals.prompt:
Create a .NET 9 inventory reservation system with the following requirements:

- An InventoryItem aggregate tracks: ProductId, AvailableQuantity, ReservedQuantity
- A Reservation entity tracks: ReservationId, ProductId, Quantity, ExpiresAt, Status (Pending/Confirmed/Expired/Cancelled)
- Business rules:
  - Cannot reserve more than available quantity
  - Reservations expire after 15 minutes if not confirmed
  - Support partial reservations (reserve what's available, return shortfall info)
  - Confirming a reservation decreases available stock permanently
  - Cancelling or expiring a reservation returns stock to available
  - Use optimistic concurrency (version/rowversion field)
- Use Result<T> for operations, value objects where appropriate
- Include xUnit tests covering normal flow, edge cases, and concurrency scenarios
- Put domain in Domain/ folder, tests in Tests/

Write all files to the outputs directory.
evals.expected_output: Aggregate with concurrency control, reservation lifecycle, Result<T>, comprehensive tests
